Package com.wip.controller

Class ShipmentController

java.lang.Object
com.wip.controller.ShipmentController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/shipments") public class ShipmentController extends Object
REST controller for managing shipments in the Courier Tracking System.

Exposes RESTful endpoints under /api/shipments that allow authorised clients to create, retrieve, update, and delete shipments. A shipment is generated for an existing parcel and is assigned a unique tracking number upon creation. All responses are serialised as JSON and the API is documented via SpringDoc / OpenAPI annotations. This controller shares the /api/shipments base path with TrackingUpdateController, which handles tracking-update sub-resources.

Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dharshan K S (dharshan.ks@wipro.com)

  • Constructor Details

    • ShipmentController

      public ShipmentController(ShipmentService shipmentService)
      Constructs a ShipmentController with the required ShipmentService.
      Parameters:
      shipmentService - the service bean responsible for shipment business logic; injected automatically by Spring's dependency injection container

  • Method Details

    • addShipment

      @PostMapping("/addShipment/{parcelid}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ShipmentDto> addShipment(@PathVariable Long parcelid)
      Creates a new shipment for an existing parcel.

      Initiates a shipment record for the parcel identified by parcelid. The service layer generates a unique tracking number and sets the initial shipment status. Only one shipment may exist per parcel; attempting to create a duplicate results in an IllegalStateException from the service layer.

      Parameters:
      parcelid - the unique identifier of the parcel for which the shipment is being created; must refer to an existing, un-shipped parcel
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the created ShipmentDto with HTTP 200
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no parcel exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)
      IllegalStateException - if a shipment already exists for the given parcel

    • getAllShipments

      @GetMapping("/getAll") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ShipmentDto>> getAllShipments()
      Retrieves a list of all shipments currently registered in the system.

      Returns every shipment record in the database, each mapped to a ShipmentDto. An empty list is returned when no shipments exist.

      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing a List of ShipmentDto objects representing all shipments, with HTTP 200

    • getShipmentById

      @GetMapping("/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ShipmentDto> getShipmentById(@PathVariable Long id)
      Retrieves the details of a single shipment identified by its unique ID.

      Looks up the shipment with the given id. If no record is found, the service layer throws a ResourceNotFoundException, resulting in an HTTP 404.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the shipment to retrieve; must be a positive Long
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the matching ShipmentDto with HTTP 200
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no shipment exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)

    • getShipmentByTrackingNumber

      @GetMapping("/tracking/{trackingNumber}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ShipmentDto> getShipmentByTrackingNumber(@PathVariable String trackingNumber)
      Retrieves a shipment using its unique tracking number.

      Provides a public-facing lookup endpoint that allows users and external systems to fetch shipment details by supplying the tracking number that was generated at shipment creation time.

      Parameters:
      trackingNumber - the unique alphanumeric tracking number assigned to the shipment
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the matching ShipmentDto with HTTP 200
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no shipment exists with the given tracking number (HTTP 404)

    • updateShipmentLocation

      @PutMapping("/{id}/location") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ShipmentDto> updateShipmentLocation(@PathVariable Long id, @RequestParam String currentLocation)
      Updates the current geographic location of a shipment in transit.

      Accepts a plain-text currentLocation request parameter and persists it against the shipment identified by id. This endpoint is intended for lightweight location-only updates, as opposed to the full-update operation.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the shipment whose location is to be updated
      currentLocation - the new current location string (e.g. city name or hub identifier)
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the updated ShipmentDto with HTTP 200
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no shipment exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)

    • deleteShipment

      @DeleteMapping("/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> deleteShipment(@PathVariable Long id)
      Permanently deletes the shipment identified by the given ID.

      Removes the shipment record from the system along with any associated tracking updates (cascaded by the service layer). On successful deletion, a 204 No Content response is returned with an empty body.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the shipment to delete; must be a positive Long
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with no body and HTTP 204 on success
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no shipment exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)
